items.title: Quote-to-cash scenario test spec
items.description: Drive quotation -> sales order -> delivery -> sales invoice -> customer payment, asserting business invariants at each step: stock decreases on delivery, AR increases on invoice, AR returns to zero on payment, and the GL stays balanced throughout.
items.applicability: End-to-end validation of a generated order-to-cash flow.
items.example: Steps: create quote, convert to order, deliver (assert -qty in stock ledger), invoice (assert +AR and balanced GL), receive payment (assert AR settled, Cash debited).
items.limitation: Assumes goods are stocked and priced; the scenario does not cover backorder, partial delivery, or partial payment unless explicitly extended with those steps and assertions.

items.title: Procure-to-pay scenario test spec
items.description: Drive purchase requisition -> purchase order (approved) -> goods receipt -> purchase invoice -> supplier payment, asserting stock increases on receipt, AP increases on invoice, AP clears on payment, and the GL is balanced.
items.applicability: End-to-end validation of a generated purchase-to-pay flow.
items.example: Steps: requisition, PO with approval, receive (assert +qty and inventory valuation), invoice (assert +AP), pay (assert AP cleared, Cash credited).
items.limitation: Three-way match (PO vs receipt vs invoice) tolerance is policy-specific; the scenario must state the tolerance it asserts or it cannot distinguish an over-invoice from a rounding difference.

items.title: Month-end journal entry scenario test spec
items.description: Post period-end adjusting journal entries (accruals, depreciation, prepayments), then assert the trial balance still balances, the entries fall in the intended open period, and posting into a closed period is rejected.
items.applicability: Period-close validation in a generated accounting module.
items.example: Post a depreciation JE dated period-end, assert trial-balance debits == credits, then attempt a JE into a closed prior period and assert rejection.
items.limitation: Does not validate the correctness of the accrual amounts themselves; it validates posting integrity and period control, not the business judgement behind the figures.

items.title: Customer onboarding with approval scenario test spec
items.description: Create a customer master record in Draft, route it through the approval workflow, and assert that the customer cannot be transacted against until approved, that the AR control account links correctly, and that the approval is recorded in the audit trail.
items.applicability: Master-data governance validation in a generated ERP.
items.example: Create customer (Draft), attempt to invoice -> rejected; approve customer; invoice succeeds; assert audit row for the approval.
items.limitation: Duplicate-customer detection (by tax id/name) is a separate control; this scenario validates the approval gate, not de-duplication, unless extended.

items.title: Supplier onboarding with approval scenario test spec
items.description: Create a supplier master in Draft, route through approval, and assert that purchase documents cannot reference an unapproved supplier, that bank-detail changes are themselves controlled, and that approvals are audited.
items.applicability: Supplier master governance validation in a generated ERP.
items.example: Create supplier (Draft), attempt PO -> rejected; approve; PO succeeds; change supplier bank details -> requires re-approval; assert audit rows.
items.limitation: Bank-detail change control is a fraud-prevention concern that may need maker-checker beyond the onboarding gate; the scenario must add that step explicitly to assert it.

items.title: High-value payment approval scenario test spec
items.description: Submit a payment above the configured high-value threshold and assert it requires the additional senior approver, that a different maker and checker are enforced, that a below-threshold payment follows the shorter path, and that all decisions are audited.
items.example: Payment of 15000 (threshold 10000) requires Finance Manager + Director; self-approval rejected; payment of 500 auto-approved; assert audit trail for each.
items.limitation: Threshold comparison must be in base currency; a scenario that submits a foreign-currency amount must assert the converted value crossed the band, not the raw amount.

items.title: BOM to work-order scenario test spec
items.description: Define a bill of materials, create a work order from it, consume raw materials, and produce the finished good. Assert that raw-material stock decreases by BOM quantities, finished-good stock increases, and the production cost rolls up from consumed material value.
items.applicability: Manufacturing module validation in a generated ERP.
items.example: BOM: 2x A + 1x B -> 1x FG. Work order qty 5 consumes 10 A and 5 B, produces 5 FG; assert stock movements and FG valuation = consumed cost / 5.
items.limitation: Ignores routing, operation time, and overhead absorption; cost roll-up here is material-only and must state that scope or it overstates accuracy.

items.title: Quality inspection failure scenario test spec
items.description: Receive goods that require inspection, fail the inspection, and assert that the rejected quantity is not added to available stock (held in a quarantine/rejected state) and that downstream consumption cannot draw from rejected stock.
items.applicability: Incoming-quality-control validation in a generated ERP.
items.example: Receive 10, inspect, reject 3; assert available stock +7, quarantine +3; attempt to issue from quarantine -> rejected.
items.limitation: Quarantine handling requires a distinct stock state or warehouse; without it, a failed inspection cannot be modelled and the scenario cannot be asserted.

items.title: Support ticket escalation scenario test spec
items.description: Create a support ticket, breach its response SLA, and assert the ticket escalates to a higher tier/assignee, that the escalation is recorded, and that the SLA timer and status transitions follow the configured policy.
items.applicability: Helpdesk/service module validation in a generated ERP.
items.example: Open ticket with 4h SLA; advance clock past SLA; assert status=Escalated, assignee changed to tier-2, escalation audit row written.
items.limitation: SLA tests depend on a controllable clock; relying on real elapsed time makes the scenario slow and flaky, so the generator must expose a time abstraction.

items.title: Asset maintenance scenario test spec
items.description: Register a fixed asset, schedule preventive maintenance, complete a maintenance work order, and assert that maintenance cost is captured against the asset, the next-due date advances, and (if applicable) depreciation continues unaffected.
items.applicability: Asset-management module validation in a generated ERP.
items.example: Create asset, schedule monthly maintenance, complete one cycle; assert maintenance log entry, cost posted, next-due = +1 month.
items.limitation: Capitalising versus expensing maintenance cost is an accounting-policy choice; the scenario must state which it asserts or it cannot verify correct GL impact.

items.title: HR onboarding scenario test spec
items.description: Onboard an employee through application/offer -> employee master creation -> approval -> assignment to a payroll structure, asserting the employee cannot appear in payroll until approved and that role/permission provisioning follows the configured policy.
items.applicability: HR/payroll module validation in a generated ERP.
items.example: Create employee (Draft), run payroll -> employee excluded; approve employee and assign salary structure; run payroll -> employee included with correct gross.
items.limitation: Payroll calculation correctness (tax, deductions) is a separate concern; this scenario validates the onboarding gate and inclusion, not the payroll arithmetic.

items.title: POS sale scenario test spec
items.description: Ring up a point-of-sale transaction, take payment, and assert that stock decreases for the sold items, revenue and tax post to the GL, the cash/till balance increases, and the sale is balanced and immutable once closed.
items.applicability: POS/retail module validation in a generated ERP.
items.example: Sell 2x item at 10 + 15% tax; assert stock -2, revenue +20, tax +3, till +23, GL balanced; attempt to edit closed sale -> rejected.
items.limitation: Offline/queued POS sync and till reconciliation at shift end are additional concerns not covered unless the scenario explicitly adds a close-shift step.

items.title: Multi-company transaction scenario test spec
items.description: Operate two companies in one ERP and assert that documents are scoped to their company, reports never leak across companies, and an inter-company stock transfer posts a matched sale and purchase with an inter-company reconciliation account that nets to zero.
items.applicability: Multi-company isolation and inter-company validation in a generated ERP.
items.example: Company X transfers stock to Company Y; assert X books a sale, Y books a purchase, inter-company account nets to zero, and a Company-X report shows no Company-Y rows.
items.limitation: Inter-company currency differences and elimination at consolidation are advanced concerns; this scenario asserts isolation and matched posting, not consolidated reporting.

items.title: Credit-limit enforcement scenario test spec
items.description: Set a customer credit limit, raise orders/invoices toward it, and assert that an order which would push outstanding AR over the limit is blocked or routed for override approval, and that settling an invoice frees credit again.
items.applicability: Credit-control validation in a generated sales module.
items.example: Limit 1000; invoice 800 (ok); attempt order of 400 -> blocked/override; receive 800 payment; retry order -> allowed.
items.limitation: Credit exposure may include open orders as well as invoices; the scenario must state whether the limit checks invoiced AR only or also committed orders, since results differ.
